title: Delayed Threat Processing in EU Region

communications.message: We are investigating reports of delayed threat processing for customers in the EU region. Threats are still being detected and queued for analysis, but processing times are slower than normal. Customers in the US and Asia-Pacific regions are not affected. Our team is actively investigating the cause and we will provide an update within 30 minutes.

We have identified a database infrastructure issue affecting threat processing in the EU region. Our team has initiated failover to backup infrastructure to restore normal processing speeds. Threat detection remains fully operational and all threats are being queued safely with no data loss. We expect processing times to return to normal within the next 2 hours and will provide an update in 30 minutes.

Threat processing speeds in the EU region are returning to normal following infrastructure adjustments. We are monitoring the recovery closely and confirming that queued threats are being processed. All threat detection functionality remained operational throughout this incident. We will provide a final update once we confirm full resolution.

This incident has been resolved. Threat processing in the EU region has returned to normal speeds and all queued threats have been processed. We apologize for any inconvenience this may have caused.

Incident Summary:
- Start time: Approximately 7:25 AM PT
- Resolution time: 9:45 AM PT
- Duration: 2 hours 20 minutes
- Impact: EU region customers experienced delayed threat processing; all threats were detected and queued with no data loss
